title: Global Markets Shaken as AI Investment Concerns Grow
subtitle: Investors question if the massive spending on artificial intelligence can truly deliver long-term profits.

summary.en: Global stock markets have experienced a sudden drop as investors worry about the high costs of AI technology. Major technology companies are facing pressure to prove that their expensive AI projects will actually make money. Some analysts compare the current situation to the dot-com bubble of the early 2000s. Meanwhile, companies like Oracle are cutting jobs to fund their massive infrastructure needs. The future of the tech sector remains uncertain as experts debate whether this is a temporary pause or a larger problem.
summary.zh: 全球股市經歷了突如其來的下跌，投資人擔憂人工智慧技術的高昂成本。大型科技公司面臨壓力，必須證明其昂貴的人工智慧專案確實能獲利。一些分析師將當前局勢與 2000 年代初期的網路泡沫相提並論。與此同時，像甲骨文這樣的公司正在裁員，以資助其龐大的基礎設施需求。科技產業的未來仍充滿不確定性，專家們正在爭論這是一次暫時的停頓，還是一個更大的問題。

paragraphs.en: Financial markets around the world received a sharp wake-up call this week. After months of rising stock prices, a wave of selling hit major technology companies. This sudden change has caused many people to doubt if the current excitement around artificial intelligence (AI) can continue. The tech-focused Nasdaq index fell significantly, and international chipmakers also saw their values drop. This decline suggests that investors may have moved too quickly to fund the hardware and infrastructure behind the AI shift.
paragraphs.zh: 全球金融市場本週收到了一記強烈的警鐘。在經歷了數月的股價上漲後，一波拋售潮襲擊了大型科技公司。這種突如其來的轉變讓許多人開始懷疑，目前圍繞人工智慧（AI）的熱潮是否能夠持續。以科技股為主的納斯達克指數大幅下跌，國際晶片製造商的市值也隨之縮水。這波跌勢顯示，投資人可能過於倉促地投入資金，以支持人工智慧轉型背後的硬體與基礎設施。

paragraphs.en: For the past three months, stock exchanges have climbed on pure optimism. Many indices reached record highs as money poured into AI technology. However, this long period of growth left stock prices looking very expensive. According to the BBC, market watchers are now questioning whether the actual use of AI by companies can justify these high prices. The downturn hit semiconductor companies, such as Nvidia and Intel, particularly hard.
paragraphs.zh: 過去三個月以來，股市在純粹的樂觀情緒下不斷攀升。隨著資金湧入人工智慧技術，許多指數創下歷史新高。然而，這段長期的成長期導致股價看起來過於昂貴。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，市場觀察家現在正質疑，企業對人工智慧的實際應用是否足以支撐這些高昂的價格。這波跌勢對輝達（Nvidia）和英特爾（Intel）等半導體公司造成了特別沉重的打擊。

paragraphs.en: This shift in mood has spread to other high-profile assets. For example, SpaceX, which recently became a public company, has faced a very difficult time in the stock market. According to The Guardian, the company’s share price dropped after its initial public offering (IPO)—the first time a company sells its shares to the public. While some traders see the recent price changes as a normal part of the market, others worry that it shows how speculative, or risky, today’s investments have become.
paragraphs.zh: 這種情緒的轉變已經蔓延到其他備受矚目的資產。例如，最近成為公開上市公司的太空探索技術公司（SpaceX），在股市中面臨了非常艱難的時刻。根據衛報（The Guardian）報導，該公司在首次公開募股（IPO）——即公司首次向大眾出售股份——之後，股價便出現下跌。雖然一些交易員將近期的價格變動視為市場的正常現象，但其他人則擔心這顯示出當前的投資已變得多麼投機或具有風險。

paragraphs.en: There are growing concerns that the massive spending on AI is becoming a bubble. A bubble happens when the price of something rises far above its real value, eventually leading to a sudden crash. Some economists are comparing this to the dot-com bubble that burst in the early 2000s. The Guardian reports that seven major tech companies now make up 30% of the S&P 500’s value. This heavy reliance on a few companies makes investors nervous about what might happen if the trend changes.
paragraphs.zh: 外界越來越擔心，對人工智慧的大規模支出正在形成泡沫。泡沫是指某樣東西的價格遠高於其實際價值，最終導致崩盤。一些經濟學家將此與 2000 年代初期破裂的網路泡沫進行比較。衛報報導指出，七家大型科技公司目前佔據了標準普爾 500 指數（S&P 500）總價值的 30%。這種對少數公司的嚴重依賴，讓投資人對於趨勢一旦改變可能發生的後果感到不安。

paragraphs.en: Adding to the pressure, companies are taking on large amounts of debt to pay for their AI projects. Debt is money that a company borrows and must pay back later. Ars Technica reports that Oracle, a large software company, recently announced 21,000 job cuts. The company explained that the use of AI in its operations has led to these reductions. At the same time, Oracle plans to raise billions of dollars to build data centers, with much of that money coming from new debt. This strategy has worried some investors, who fear that companies are spending too much money they do not have.
paragraphs.zh: 雪上加霜的是，企業正背負巨額債務來支付人工智慧專案的費用。債務是指企業借入並必須在未來償還的資金。科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導稱，大型軟體公司甲骨文（Oracle）最近宣布裁員 21,000 人。該公司解釋，人工智慧在其營運中的應用導致了這些人力精簡。與此同時，甲骨文計畫籌集數十億美元來建設資料中心，其中大部分資金來自於新債務。這項策略讓一些投資人感到擔憂，他們害怕企業正在花費太多自己根本沒有的錢。

paragraphs.en: Economic factors are also playing a role in this uncertainty. The Federal Reserve has hinted that it may increase interest rates. When interest rates go up, the cost of borrowing money increases, which makes it harder for companies to fund their expensive projects. According to The Guardian, Ipek Ozkardeskaya, a senior analyst at Swissquote, noted that AI-related borrowing is expected to reach over $500 billion this year. This high level of borrowing is causing concern among analysts who fear that Big Tech is relying too much on debt to fuel its growth.
paragraphs.zh: 經濟因素也在這種不確定性中扮演了角色。美國聯準會（Federal Reserve）已暗示可能會提高利率。當利率上升時，借貸成本隨之增加，這使得企業更難為其昂貴的專案籌措資金。根據衛報報導，瑞士證券（Swissquote）的資深分析師奧茲卡德塞卡亞（Ipek Ozkardeskaya）指出，預計今年與人工智慧相關的借貸將達到 5,000 億美元以上。這種高水準的借貸引起了分析師的擔憂，他們害怕大型科技公司過度依賴債務來推動成長。

paragraphs.en: Market experts are now divided on what will happen next. Some believe this sell-off is just a healthy, temporary pause after a period of historic growth. Vivek Arya, an analyst at Bank of America, suggested that as demand for AI technology grows, the industry will eventually move toward solving physical infrastructure and power needs, which could help forecasts improve. On the other hand, many sceptics argue that the era of easy market gains is over, especially as corporate budgets for IT spending begin to cool down.
paragraphs.zh: 市場專家對於接下來會發生什麼事意見分歧。有些人認為，這波拋售只是在經歷歷史性成長後，一次健康且暫時的停頓。美國銀行（Bank of America）分析師艾里亞（Vivek Arya）表示，隨著對人工智慧技術的需求成長，產業最終將轉向解決實體基礎設施與電力需求，這可能有助於改善預測。另一方面，許多懷疑論者則主張，市場輕鬆獲利的時代已經結束，特別是企業的資訊科技支出預算已開始降溫。

paragraphs.en: As the week continues, Wall Street will be watching upcoming corporate earnings reports very closely. These reports will show if tech giants are actually making real profits from their AI investments or if they are just creating marketing buzz. While some markets, like London’s FTSE 100, have remained steady, the global tech sector is clearly entering a more cautious phase. Whether this leads to a larger retreat or a new period of stability remains to be seen.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著本週持續進行，華爾街將密切關注即將發布的企業財報。這些報告將顯示科技巨頭是否真的從人工智慧投資中獲得了實質利潤，還是僅僅在製造行銷話題。雖然倫敦金融時報 100 指數（FTSE 100）等部分市場保持穩定，但全球科技產業顯然已進入一個更加謹慎的階段。這是否會導致更大規模的退場，還是進入一個新的穩定時期，仍有待觀察。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An event that warns someone that they need to change their way of doing things.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 警訊；敲響警鐘（提醒人們需要改變現狀的事件）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思是「叫醒電話」，這裡指市場發出的警訊。文中：Financial markets around the world received a sharp wake-up call this week.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation where the price of something rises far above its real value, eventually leading to a sudden crash.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 泡沫（指價格遠高於實際價值，最終會崩盤的經濟現象）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（肥皂泡），這裡指經濟泡沫。文中：There are growing concerns that the massive spending on AI is becoming a bubble.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To agree to be responsible for something, such as debt or work.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「接納」或「對抗」，這裡指承擔債務。文中：Adding to the pressure, companies are taking on large amounts of debt to pay for their AI projects.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become less active or intense.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 冷卻；趨緩（指活動或成長速度變慢）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「降溫（物理溫度）」，這裡指預算成長趨緩。文中：On the other hand, many sceptics argue that the era of easy market gains is over, especially as corporate budgets for IT spending begin to cool down.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Tech stocks tumble on concerns over AI spending

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: US AI stock sell-off shakes markets from Wall Street to Asia

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments

quiz.question: According to the article, what is one specific action Oracle is taking that has caused concern among investors?

options.A: They are reducing their total number of data centers.
options.B: They are borrowing significant amounts of money to fund infrastructure.
options.C: They are selling off their AI software division to competitors.
options.D: They are increasing their dividend payments despite low profits.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第五段，Oracle 宣布裁員 21,000 人，同時計劃籌集數十億美元來建設數據中心，且大部分資金來自於新債務（new debt）。選項 A 錯誤，因為他們是在建設而非減少數據中心；選項 C 文中未提及；選項 D 與文中提到的債務問題無關。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between interest rates and the AI investment boom?

options.A: Higher interest rates encourage companies to invest more in AI hardware.
options.B: The AI boom is likely to accelerate if the Federal Reserve lowers interest rates.
options.C: Rising interest rates make it more expensive for tech companies to sustain their current AI spending.
options.D: The Federal Reserve is intentionally trying to stop all AI development through high rates.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段，聯準會暗示可能提高利率，而利率上升會增加借貸成本，使得企業更難為昂貴的項目提供資金。這暗示了若利率持續上升，依賴舉債進行 AI 擴張的科技公司將面臨更大的財務壓力。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 雖有邏輯關聯，但文中並未直接討論降息的影響；選項 D 過度推論，文中僅提到利率對借貸成本的影響，而非針對 AI 的針對性打壓。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'cool' mean in the phrase 'corporate budgets for IT spending begin to cool down'?

options.A: To become less enthusiastic or decrease in intensity.
options.B: To lower the temperature of a physical space.
options.C: To become more fashionable or trendy.
options.D: To act in a calm and collected manner.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，「cool down」是用來形容 IT 預算增長速度放緩或熱度減退，不再像過去那樣激進。選項 B 是字面意思，但在商業語境中不適用；選項 C 與預算減少的含義衝突；選項 D 描述的是人的態度，而非預算趨勢。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the current state of the AI market?

options.A: The AI industry has officially collapsed, leading to a global economic recession.
options.B: Investors are universally confident that AI will provide massive returns in the near future.
options.C: Tech companies have successfully proven that AI investments are already highly profitable.
options.D: There is growing uncertainty and caution regarding whether AI investments are sustainable or overvalued.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。全文的核心在於探討市場對 AI 熱潮的擔憂，包括高估值、過度舉債以及對 AI 實際獲利能力的質疑。選項 A 過於誇大，文中僅提到市場修正與擔憂；選項 B 與文中提到的市場分歧與擔憂矛盾；選項 C 與文中提到的「企業是否能從 AI 獲得真實利潤」的疑問不符。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
